name: Livraria Lello
description: One of the most beautiful bookstores in the world hides a neo-Gothic interior behind an art nouveau facade.

name: Quinta da Regaleira
description: This eccentrically decorated palace is replete with grottoes, fountains, tunnels, and caves.

name: Carmo Convent Ruins
description: Legacy of the earthquake that nearly wiped Lisbon off the face of the Earth.

name: Pavilhão Chinês
description: Ring the bell to enter a bar that looks equal parts museum and old curiosity shop.

name: Livraria Bertrand
description: The world's oldest bookshop still in operation.

name: Portugal's Chapel of Bones
description: A 16th century chapel decorated with bones, skulls, and entire bodies hanging from the wall.

name: The Village of Monsanto
description: Houses are tucked between, on, and underneath giant boulders.

name: Pena National Palace
description: This unreal Portuguese palace looks as though it's made from a pile of different castles.

name: Caza das Vellas Loreto
description: This charming candle shop has been in business since 1789.

name: Estufa Fria
description: This once-forgotten oasis in the middle of Lisbon was first created over a century ago quite by accident.

name: Igreja de São Francisco
description: The stunning interior of this Gothic church is covered in ornate gold details.

name: Capela de Ossos Bone Chapel
description: This small chapel is built of human bones and decorated with a golden skeleton.

name: Benagil Caves
description: Perhaps the most spectacular grotto along the Algarve coastline.

name: Rhinoceros of Belém Tower
description: A king's pet, a pope's gift, an artist's inspiration, and a tragic shipwreck.

name: Santa Justa Lift
description: This Industrial Age architectural and engineering gem helps visitors and residents alike traverse Lisbon, “The City of Seven Hills.”

name: Covão dos Conchos
description: This gaping hole in the middle of a remote mountain lake looks like a portal to another dimension.

name: Jerónimos Monastery
description: Elaborately carved to showcase the glorious wealth that the age of exploration brought to the Portuguese empire.

name: McDonald's Imperial
description: This restored Portuguese cafe may well be the most beautiful place in the world to grab a Big Mac.

name: Castelo dos Mouros (Castle of the Moors)
description: An ancient and majestic fortress that played an integral role in Portugal's road to independence.

name: Bordallo Pinheiro Garden
description: A garden in Lisbon filled with giant porcelain creatures.

name: Boca do Inferno
description: A unique seaside cave where Aleister Crowley faked his own death.

name: Drowned Village of Vilarinho da Furna
description: A submerged village appears when dam levels drop.

name: Convent of the Capuchos
description: The verdant mossy ruins of a Franciscan monastery lie in the remote Sintra hills awaiting exploration.

name: Conserveira de Lisboa
description: The best place in the world to buy tinned fish.

name: Preserved Head of Diogo Alves
description: An early Portuguese serial killer's head is alarmingly well preserved in a jar at the University of Lisbon.

name: Tidal Pools of Leça da Palmeira
description: An early work of the architect Álvaro Siza Vieira, these swimming pools blend into the beach.

name: Praia da Ursa
description: In Portugal, a secluded stretch of untouched natural beauty hides at the bottom of a steep hill.

name: Capela do Senhor da Pedra
description: Picturesque intersection of witchcraft and ancient Christianity.

name: History of Lisbon Mural
description: Hidden beneath a beautiful overlook you’ll find the history of the city depicted in comic strip format next to a pay toilet.

name: Livraria Simão
description: One of the smallest bookstores on the planet.

name: Carmo and Carmelitas Churches
description: A hidden three-foot-wide house separates these two regal churches.

name: Chapel of Nossa Senhora das Vitórias
description: This fairy-tale, neo-Gothic church in the Azores is a world of magical realism.

name: Church of Santa Engrácia
description: Portugal's National Pantheon has a history of curses, collapses and financial problems.

name: Igreja de São Francisco Catacombs
description: Ancient catacombs form the foundation for this Rococo orgy of a church.

name: Electricity Museum
description: Located in the thermoelectric factory that once powered the entire city, this Portuguese museum is shockingly fascinating.

name: Violet Volcanic Tea
description: When Azorean green tea meets volcanic hot springs water, a luminous brew is born.

name: Capela dos Ossos (Chapel of Bones)
description: This unassuming chapel in Portugal plays host to a ghoulish collection of bones.

name: Convento de Cristo
description: The medieval castle built as headquarters for the Pope's secretive Knights Templar.

name: Sao Bento Station
description: Elaborate tile work in this railway station tells the story of Portugal.

name: Bom Jesus do Monte
description: A stunning 18th-century site where pilgrims ascend by a series of punishing stairs only to return via funicular.

name: Jardim Botânico do Porto (Porto Botanical Garden)
description: A small but charming botanical garden that contains many exotic plant species.

name: National Coach Museum
description: An exquisite collection of fairytale conveyances that puts most car museums to shame.

name: Hospital de Bonecas
description: Catering to broken dolls for almost 200 years, this repair shop is now a charnel house of doll parts.

name: Mina de São Domingos (Sao Domingos Mines)
description: An abandoned mine now resembles a post-apocalyptic landscape of derelict buildings and blood-red pools of water.

name: Stone House
description: A small Portuguese cottage formed between boulders in the countryside.

name: Mafra Palace Library
description: At night, bats patrol this magnificent 18th-century space in search of book-eating pests.

name: Almendres Cromlech
description: "Portugal's Stonehenge" is made up of nut-like stones aligned with the heavens.

name: Banco de Materiais (Bank of Materials)
description: Building owners can take out tiles for free at this repository for historic building materials.

name: Cemetery of Anchors
description: Dead weights honor the dead of Portugal's fishing industry.

name: Hotel Monte Palace
description: This abandoned five-star hotel is now a five-star urban exploration destination.

name: Ponte da Misarela (Misalera Bridge)
description: Local lore says the devil built this beautiful medieval bridge to help a fleeing bandit.

name: Pastéis de Belém
description: Eat the ultimate iteration of Portugal's most beloved pastry.

name: Seashell Gallery
description: In the Monastery of São Vicente resides a gallery devoted to a breathtaking collection of seashells.

name: Calouste Gulbenkian Museum
description: One man's remarkable private collection of art from around the world.

name: Ponte 516 Arouca
description: One of the world’s longest pedestrian suspension bridges.

name: Islet of Vila Franca do Campo
description: A perfectly circular lake is formed by the crater of an ancient underwater volcano.

name: A Ginjinha
description: Sip cherry liqueur at a bar dating back to 1840.

name: Church Sagrado Coração
description: Tucked away inside this concrete building is a church and its grand display of brutalist architecture.

name: As Bifanas do Afonso
description: Get in line for Lisbon’s most famous sandwich.

name: Museu da Marioneta (Museum of Puppetry)
description: This museum contains a vast array of puppets and marionettes from all over the world, featuring incredible works by Portuguese creators.

name: Montemor-o-Novo Castle
description: This crumbling Portuguese ruin overlooks the populace it once ruled.

name: 'Half Rabbit'
description: This giant sculpture made of trash is a critique of society's wastefulness.

name: Conimbriga Ruins and Museum
description: One of the largest Roman settlements in Portugal.

name: Museu da Farmácia
description: Entire apothecaries are on display at this Lisbon museum, which explores the history of medicine.

name: National Museum of Ancient Art
description: This little-known national museum houses over 40,000 works of art from across the world.

name: Palace of Estoi
description: The extravagance of Portugal’s "Pink Palace" resulted in a noble title being awarded to its owner.

name: The World’s Shortest International Bridge
description: You can cross from one country to another in a single hop on this little bridge.

name: Palace Hotel Bussaco
description: A majestic hotel for dreamers rises from the center of a fairytale wood.

name: Tomb of Inês de Castro
description: The corpse bride of Portugal.

name: Toboggan Ride from Monte to Funchal
description: Men in rubber-soled boots serve as your "brakes" on this toboggan ride Hemingway described as "exhilarating."

name: Fort of Graça
description: This star-shaped military outpost is now simply trying to protect itself from neglect.

name: Madeira Botanical Garden
description: A lush oasis full of vibrant plants and striking geometric displays.

name: Necropolis of São Miguel
description: A windswept necropolis carved into the rocks overlooks the "most Portuguese town in Portugal."

name: Casa dos Bicos
description: This unique architectural wonder is also tied to one of the 20th-century's most renowned writers.

name: Espigueiros do Soajo
description: A cluster of granite granaries perched on stilts.

name: Valongo Sanatorium
description: An abandoned tuberculosis hospital said to be haunted by the patients who died within its walls.

name: Gorreana Tea Plantation
description: Europe's oldest still-operational tea farm also boasts a spectacular view.

name: Solar Branco Gin Library
description: A 19th-century mansion houses the largest collection of gin in the European Union.

name: House-Museum of Dr. Anastácio Gonçalves
description: After the owner of this Art Nouveau home died, it was turned into a museum displaying a lifetime dedicated to art.

name: Cabo da Roca
description: One Portuguese poet described this cape, the westernmost point in mainland Europe, as "where the land ends and the sea begins."

name: Principality of Pontinha
description: A micronation on an islet along the coast of a Portuguese island.

name: Castelo de Guimarães (Guimarães Castle)
description: This castle has links to Portugal’s founding and struggles for independence.

name: Praia de São Paio
description: Iron Age runes and ruins hide in plain sight along this Portuguese beach.

name: Cinemateca Portuguesa
description: In addition to showing films, this one-screen arthouse cinema from the late 1940s is a museum of old film equipment.

name: Santíssima Trindade Church
description: This oddly shaped church resembles a rocket.

name: 'The Wedding of the Hen'
description: This tile-work piece featuring a chicken riding in carriage is both a remarkable artistic creation and a social commentary of politics and culture in 17th-century Portugal.

name: Ponta do Pargo Lighthouse
description: Portugal’s highest lighthouse has one of the most impressive focal planes in the world.

name: Museu do Fado
description: An unassuming music museum recounts the rags to riches story of a dramatic Portuguese genre.

name: Saint Maria Adelaide
description: An honorary saint of the people, Maria Adelaide became an incorrupt corpse, the clothes intact, exhaling “a strong scent of roses.”

name: Fortress of Almeida
description: The medieval town's star-shaped walls are geometrically dazzling, though not entirely impenetrable.

name: Poço da Pedreira
description: Rainwater transformed this old quarry into a peaceful pond you won't find in most tourist books.

name: Museum of the Central Hospital of Porto
description: When this former hospital pharmacy closed in 2013 it already looked like a museum, so they turned it into one.

name: Museu dos Fosforos
description: Europe's largest collection of matchbox designs is a fascinating exploration of the cultural history of the world.

name: Fort of São João Baptista (Berlengas)
description: A fortress off the West coast of Portugal that dates back to 1502.

name: Casas Típicas de Santana
description: These small, triangular, colorful houses are a staple of Madeira Island.

name: Museum of Mechanical Music
description: This architectural beauty is the home to a personal collection of more than 600 examples of mechanical musical instruments.

name: Cabo Girão Skywalk
description: This glass skywalk offers vertiginous views down the volcanic cliffs to the beach below.

name: Piódão
description: Nestled in the mountains of Portugal is a tiny village made almost entirely of schist.

name: Pegadas de Dinossáurios da Serra de Aire Natural Monument
description: The ancient sauropod tracks found in this former quarry are some of the largest in the world.

name: Museu do Pão
description: Come for the bread-themed puppet show, stay for the buffet.

name: Lazarim Carnival Celebrations
description: One of the few places in Portugal where the Celtic ritual of wearing large wooden masks is still practiced.

name: Anta de Pavia
description: Pagan monument converted to Christianity with the population.

name: Porto's Heritage Streetcar System and Museum
description: This historic streetcar system has been running since the 19th-century.

name: Mount Pico
description: As the highest point in the Mid-Atlantic range this stratovolcano offers spectacular views.

name: Dolmen Chapel of Sao Brissos
description: A neolithic tomb converted into a tiny chapel.

name: Quiosque de São Paulo
description: Step back in time at this 19th-century kiosk.

name: Semea
description: Rabanadas, the Portuguese answer to French toast, are reborn here with a glassy sugar crust and a center as light as whipped cream.

name: Algar do Carvão
description: One of few vertical lava tubes in the world accessible by foot.

name: 'Veado'
description: A deer-headed statue that brings together a 12th-century legend and big wave surfing.

name: Santuario de Nossa Senhora da Peneda
description: This chapel is the site of a yearly pilgrimage that originated during the Middle Ages.

name: The Tallest Tree in Europe
description: Nicknamed "Karri Knight," this Eucalyptus was planted in 1895 and stands at 72 meters.

name: Dólmen da Capela Senhora do Monte ( Lady of the Mount Chapel Dolmen)
description: An ancient tomb that still stands in Portugal.

name: Church of St. Dominic
description: The scorched Lisbon church that survived two earthquakes and a massive fire.

name: Igreja de Vera Cruz de Marmelar
description: A chunk of the "true cross" is held in this small Portuguese church where modern day exorcisms are said to be common place.

name: Portugal dos Pequenitos
description: The "Little Ones," show Portugal in full miniature splendor.

name: A Regaleira
description: The original version of Porto's most famous sandwich is just as good here as it was a century ago.

name: Museu Nacional da Imprensa (National Press Museum)
description: This museum is home to one of the biggest collections of printing press technology—plus a river view.

name: The Sobreiro Monumental (Monumental Cork Oak)
description: For more than two centuries, the canopy of this venerable tree has been a haven for songbirds and lovers.

name: Castle of Numão
description: An 11th-century medieval castle cascades over the town of Numão.

name: Mosteiro de Santa Maria de Seiça
description: The crumbling ruins of an abandoned 12th-century monastery.

name: Aldeia Artística (Artistic Village)
description: The walls of this small village have been turned into a living street art gallery.

name: Arpad Szenes-Vieira da Silva Foundation
description: Little-known artist couple chose a former silk factory as the location for a museum dedicated to their work and other avant-garde art.

name: Seminário de Santa Teresinha
description: A devasting 2020 fire left this historic seminary in ruins.

name: Casa Fernando Pessoa
description: A museum dedicated to one of Portugal's most celebrated writers.

name: Miradouro do Sanatório (Sanatorium Viewpoint)
description: Panoramic views from the mountains over a facility built to provide tuberculosis patients with fresh mountain air.

name: Casa Museu Fernando de Castro
description: The former home of a Portuguese poet, artist, and collector is packed to the brim with art.

name: Covão da Ametade
description: A glacial valley filled with silver beech trees in the mountains of Portugal.

name: The Bridge Collection
description: The history of drinking is told through 1,800 cups, chalices, and glasses at this exhibit.

name: Horse of Mazouco
description: This impressive rupestrian engraving dates back to the Upper Palaeolithic period.

name: Bacalhôa Buddha Eden
description: Explore a remarkable 35-hectare sculpture garden located an hour outside of Lisbon.

name: Santuário Rupestre de Panóias (Panoias Sanctuary)
description: This Gallo-Roman sanctuary is dedicated to Serápis, god of the underworld.

name: Passeio dos Poetas Stone Faces
description: On a trail in Portugal's Barão de São João National Forest, a pile of faces look up at hikers from the forest floor.

name: Casa dos Caracóis
description: Feast on buckets of garlicky snails.

name: Centum Cellas
description: This enigmatic Roman tower lurks just outside Belmonte.

name: Fajãs of São Jorge
description: Bits of flat terrain surrounded by cliffs make one of the most beautiful landscapes in the Azores.

name: Lagoa das Sete Cidades (Lagoon of the Seven Cities)
description: Legend has it the lovelorn tears of a princess and a shepherd boy formed the blue and green twin lakes in the Azores archipelago.

name: Espigueros of Lindoso
description: These narrow stone granaries have been used to store and dry out grain for hundreds of years.

name: Penascosa Petroglyph
description: These rock drawings in the Côa Valley also create an ancient optical illusion.

name: Nossa Senhora da Boa Estrela (Our Lady of the Good Star)
description: A small sanctuary carved into the mountainside near the tallest point in continental Portugal.

name: Oficina Museu (Workshop Museum)
description: One artist's collection of homemade handicrafts doubles as a monument to 20th-century material culture.

name: Planetário do Porto
description: A scientific observatory managed the largest Portuguese astronomy research unit.

name: Fortaleza de Juromenha
description: This abandoned fortress still stands guard over the Guadiana River despite a history of warfare.

name: Fórnea de Porto de Mós
description: A naturally occurring amphitheater carved by erosion into the mountainside.

name: Côa Museum
description: An architectural gem nestled into a promontory overlooking a prehistoric landscape.

name: Pan Am Billboard
description: Some 4,000 individual tiles make up this striking blue billboard from the golden age of air travel.

name: Minas dos Carris
description: An abandoned mining complex in Serra do Gerês.

name: Santuário do Sagrado Coração de Jesus
description: An icon of the city, this chapel features an amazing dome ceiling and offers panoramic views.

name: Monastery of Santa Clara-a-Velha
description: These 400-year-old ruins are all that's left of the monastery where Ines de Castro was assassinated by the King of Portugal.

name: Santa Cruz da Graciosa Bullring
description: This Portuguese bullring is built right into the caldera of an island volcano.

name: 'Plastic Mero'
description: Crafted from ocean litter, this sculpture highlights the ills of overconsumption.

name: Torre da Estrela
description: This tower once served as a radar station during the Cold War.

name: CR7 Museum
description: A shrine-like museum entirely dedicated to soccer superstar Cristiano Ronaldo.

name: Piri Piri
description: Taste the precursor of peri-peri chicken at this restaurant just outside of Lisbon.

name: Gil Eannes
description: This ship offers visitors the chance to spend the night aboard a former floating hospital for fishermen.

name: Núcleo Museológico do Sal
description: This museum documents the ancient practice of salt production in Figueira da Foz.

name: Porto Roque Border Post
description: An abandoned anti-smuggling station decays along the boundary between Portugal and Spain.

name: Cabinet of Curiosities at the University of Coimbra
description: One of the oldest universities in the world exhibits thousands of specimens from its 700-year-old collection.

name: Polychrome Augustus of Prima Porta
description: A replica of the Roman emperor in glorious full color.

name: Poças
description: Sample port and quinine-fortified wine at this fourth-generation winery near Porto.

name: Líder
description: Dine on Porto-style tripe at a restaurant run by the head of a gastronomic brotherhood dedicated to uplifting this humble dish.

name: Farol da Ponta dos Rosais
description: On the cliffs of São Jorge in the Azores stand the remains of a lighthouse that was struck by a series of earthquakes.

name: Capela de Nossa Senhora de Aracelis
description: Before this rocky hill was home to a chapel, it was used as a lookout by Romans and Moors.

name: Ponte da Ajuda (Ajuda Bridge)
description: The ruins of a 16th-century bridge loom over Olivença's sovereignty.

name: Conservas Pinhais
description: Sample some of Iberia’s best tinned fish at this century-old cannery just north of Porto.

name: Cemitério dos Prazeres (Prazeres Cemetery)
description: This historic cemetery is home to the largest private mausoleum in Europe.

name: Gabinete de Curiosidades (Cabinet of Curiosities)
description: A Portuguese museum display features a bizarre assortment of objects.

name: Saint Anthony’s Sign of the Cross
description: The devil-defying mark left by a saint as a boy is visible inside Lisbon's cathedral.

name: Chapel of Espírito Santo dos Mareantes
description: Ghostly ship drawings grace the walls of this medieval hospital.

name: Molly Williams Mural
description: In Portugal, a larger-than-life painting of America's first Black female firefighter.

name: O Museu Ibérico da Máscara e do Traje (Iberian Museum of Masks and Costumes)
description: A collection of unique, and sometimes terrifying, handmade masks and costumes used for local festivals.

name: Prometheus Statue
description: In a fusion of myth and mischief, this statue is a testament to this university's quest for both knowledge and pleasure

name: Geographical Institute of the University of Porto
description: A seismograph and a bunker from the Cold War still sit under this university building.

name: Casa do Montado
description: Don't miss the xylotheque (a library of wood) at this museum devoted to cork.

name: EFANOR Factory Ruins
description: Once a thriving textile powerhouse, now a relic of what once was.

name: Panorâmico de Monsanto
description: This now abandoned restaurant provides some of the most amazing views of Lisboa.
